examples: Zora Neale Hurston is a great novelist, folklorist, and anthropologist in America.
Near the end of his life, folklorist Alan Lomax recorded a series of interviews with the pianist.
American folklorist Alan Lomax suggested that "Shenandoah" was a sea-shanty and that its "composers" quite possibly were French-Canadian voyageurs.
In 1941, the folklorist Alan Lomax travelled throughout the Deep South, recording local musicians on behalf of the Library of Congress.
